journal_entry.description: Purchase of tea, beverages, and refreshments for client entertainment
journal_entry.rationale: Client entertainment expense increases (debit expense). Sales tax is added to the expense base. Corporate credit card creates a liability (credit liability). In Canada, businesses can typically claim Input Tax Credits (ITCs) on GST/HST for business expenses, but entertainment expenses have special rules - only 50% of meals and entertainment is deductible for income tax purposes, though the full GST/HST ITC may be claimable. For simplicity and following common practice for client entertainment, we record the full amount including tax to Entertainment Expense, with the liability to the credit card company.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Client entertainment is an operating expense. The $1,200 base plus $120 tax (10%) totaling $1,320 increases Entertainment Expense (debit), and the corporate credit card creates a liability (credit). One expense increase, one liability increase.

output_credit_classifier.reason: Client entertainment is an operating expense. The $1,200 base plus $120 tax (10%) totaling $1,320 increases Entertainment Expense (debit), and the corporate credit card creates a liability (credit). One expense increase, one liability increase.
